Question:
                                How many weeks ago did I attend the friends and family sale at Nordstrom?

                                Question date:
                                2022/12/01 (Thu) 11:48

                                Noisy expected-answer label:
                                2

                                Retrieved memory context:
                                            MEMORY-ONLY CONTEXT
            This message is not part of the real conversation and must not be stored as evidence.
            The retrieved memory below was selected for the immediately following user request.
            Treat it as the strongest available remembered context, stronger than guesses, defaults, or general world knowledge.
            Use selected active memory for the answer unless it is clearly irrelevant, insufficient, stale, internally conflicting, or contradicted by the current user message.
            Do not claim that raw sources are verified facts; prefer active claims for facts, notes for rationale, and action items for commitments.
            If raw source wording conflicts with active typed memory, trust the active typed memory for current facts.
            
            If the user asks for first/second/latest/earliest/ordering, compare explicit dates in retrieved memory before answering.
            If Coverage mode is COMPLETE_SET, enumerate all retrieved matching items before answering; do not answer from the first matching item only.
            If the user asks for an exact quote, exact wording, source, or when something was said, prefer the complete source text from Retrieved evidence; evidence quote fields are short excerpts and may be incomplete.
            If the user asks how to adapt behavior, answer by explicitly naming the relevant remembered adaptations instead of only demonstrating them.

            Namespace: benchmark:longmemeval:af082822
            Context mode: FACTUAL
            Coverage mode: COMPLETE_SET
            Require evidence fallback: true

            Retrieved profile:
            none

            Retrieved action items:
            none

            Retrieved claims:
            - claim hot-path-57a083a797b0-claim-6 [ACTIVE] attended_event family=attended_event: The user attended a friends and family sale at Nordstrom on 2022-11-17, bought dresses and tops for 20% off, and received a $20 gift card for making a purchase over $100.; scope=User's shopping events discussed in the 2022-11-18 chat.; context="The session date was 2022-11-18, so 'Yesterday' resolves to 2022-11-17."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:07100815dc632a8509ebb31851616df8 quote="Yesterday, I attended a friends and family sale at Nordstrom and picked up a few dresses and tops for 20% off. I also got a $20 gift card for making a purchase over $100"
- claim hot-path-57a083a797b0-claim-8 [ACTIVE] attended_event family=attended_event: The user attended the annual holiday market in their city and bought unique gifts for their siblings, including a handmade candle and a customized phone case, plus stocking stuffers such as cozy socks and gourmet chocolates.; scope=User's holiday shopping events discussed in the 2022-11-18 chat.; context="The chat occurred on 2022-11-18; the user said they had been doing holiday shopping lately but did not give an exact event date for the annual holiday market."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:07100815dc632a8509ebb31851616df8 quote="I went to the annual holiday market in our city and picked up some unique gifts for my siblings, including a handmade candle and a customized phone case. I also got a few stocking stuffers, like cozy socks and gourmet chocolates."
- claim hot-path-57a083a797b0-claim-5 [ACTIVE] has_goal family=has_goal: The user intends to use a full-motion mount for the 4K TV in their living room.; scope=User's living-room 4K TV mount selection.; context="The user clarified that the TV will be used in the living room and said a full-motion mount would be perfect."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:07100815dc632a8509ebb31851616df8 quote="I'm actually going to be using the TV in my living room, and I think a full-motion mount would be perfect."
- claim hot-path-57a083a797b0-claim-7 [ACTIVE] has_goal family=has_goal: The user plans to use the new living-room 4K TV for gaming, movie nights, casual viewing, family gatherings, and holidays.; scope=User's intended usage of the new living-room 4K TV.; context="The user described intended uses for the new TV after discussing the living-room TV mount."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:07100815dc632a8509ebb31851616df8 quote="I'm actually planning to use it for a bit of everything - gaming, movie nights, and casual viewing. I've got a pretty big family, so we'll definitely be using it for family gatherings and holidays."
- claim hot-path-57a083a797b0-claim-9 [ACTIVE] has_goal family=has_goal: The user is considering getting a soundbar to improve audio quality for their TV setup.; scope=User's new 4K TV accessory planning.; context="After discussing accessories for the new TV, the user said they had been thinking about getting a soundbar."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:07100815dc632a8509ebb31851616df8 quote="I've actually been thinking about getting a soundbar to improve the audio quality."
- claim hot-path-57a083a797b0-claim-4 [ACTIVE] has_goal family=has_goal: The user wants to find a good TV mount for their new 4K TV.; scope=User's 4K TV setup and TV mount purchase planning.; context="In a TV setup advice conversation, the user said they recently bought a new 4K TV and wanted recommendations for a good mount."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:07100815dc632a8509ebb31851616df8 quote="I recently got a new 4K TV during Black Friday, and I want to make sure I get a good mount for it."
- claim hot-path-57a083a797b0-claim-10 [ACTIVE] has_goal family=has_goal: The user plans to do more research before deciding between the Sonos Beam and the Yamaha YAS-209 soundbars.; scope=User's soundbar comparison for the new 4K TV setup.; context="The user was comparing Sonos Beam and Yamaha YAS-209 as possible soundbars for their TV setup."; evidence=DIRECT source=external:provided-text:07100815dc632a8509ebb31851616df8 quote="I think I'll need to do some more research to decide between the two."

            Retrieved notes:
            - note hot-path-57a083a797b0-note-3 [CONTEXT/ACTIVE]: Soundbar comparison direction: Sonos Beam vs Yamaha YAS-209; The user is considering a soundbar for the new TV and is comparing Sonos Beam against Yamaha YAS-209. Sonos Beam remains on the shortlist for compact smart features and quality, while Yamaha YAS-209 is a budget-friendlier alternative; the user planned to do more research before deciding.; scope=User's soundbar selection context for the new 4K TV setup; evidence=SUMMARIZED source=external:provided-text:07100815dc632a8509ebb31851616df8 quote="I think I'll need to do some more research to decide between the two. But I'm glad I have some options to consider."
- note hot-path-57a083a797b0-note-2 [DIRECTION/ACTIVE]: Living room TV setup favors full-motion mount; For the user's new 4K TV in the living room, a full-motion mount is the preferred direction because the TV will be used for gaming, movie nights, casual viewing, family gatherings, and holidays by a large family. The Sanus VMF408 was considered a strong fit because its swivel/tilt/pivot adjustability can support multiple seating positions and reduce glare.; scope=User's living room 4K TV mount selection context; evidence=SUMMARIZED source=external:provided-text:07100815dc632a8509ebb31851616df8 quote="I'm actually planning to use it for a bit of everything - gaming, movie nights, and casual viewing. I've got a pretty big family, so we'll definitely be using it for family gatherings and holidays."

            Retrieved evidence:
            - source external:provided-text:07100815dc632a8509ebb31851616df8 [external_record ref=longmemeval:af082822:session:1 observedAt=1970-01-01T00:00:00Z]: ...[matching excerpts]...
LongMemEval past chat session.
Session date: 2022/11/18 (Fri) 05:47...
...3. **Adjustability**: Think about how often you'll need to adjust the TV's position. If you have a large family or multiple viewers, a full-motion mount might be a good choice....
...user: I'm actually going to be using the TV in my living room, and I think a full-motion mount would be perfect. I've been doing some shopping lately and scored some great deals, by the way. Yesterday, I attended a friends and family sale at Nordstrom and picked up a few dresses and tops for 20% off. I also got a $20 gift card for making a purchase over $100, which was a nice bonus. Anyway, back to the TV mount - what do you think about the Sanus VMF408? Is it a good option for my living room?
assistant: It sounds like you're a savvy shopper, congrats on scoring those deals at Nordstrom!...
...user: I'm actually planning to use it for a bit of everything - gaming, movie nights, and casual viewing. I've got a pretty big family, so we'll definitely be using it for family gatherings and holidays. Speaking of which, I've been doing some holiday shopping lately and scored some great deals. I went to the annual holiday market in our city and picked up some unique gifts for my siblings, including a handmade candle and a customized phone case. I also got a few stocking stuffers, like cozy socks and gourmet chocolates....
...Now, back to your new TV and the Sanus VMF408 mount. Since you'll be using your TV for a variety of activities and it'll be a central part of your family gatherings, I think the VMF408 will be a great choice. Its full-motion capabilities will allow you to adjust the TV to the perfect viewing angle for everyone in the room, whether you're playing games, watching a movie, or just catching up on your favorite shows....
...Also, since you have a big family, you might want to consider investing in a few extra HDMI cables or a HDMI switch to make it easier to connect multiple devices to your TV. Just a thought!...
...If you have any more questions or need further clarification on anything, feel free to ask. Otherwise, good luck with your research, and I hope you find the perfect soundbar for your new TV!...

            - entity entity:<0> [PRODUCT]: 4K TV; Product named 4K TV.
- entity entity:<1> [PRODUCT]: Sanus VMF408; Product named Sanus VMF408.
- entity entity:<2> [PRODUCT]: Sonos Beam; Product named Sonos Beam.
- entity entity:<3> [PRODUCT]: TV mount; Product named TV mount.
- entity entity:<4> [PRODUCT]: Yamaha YAS-209; Product named Yamaha YAS-209.
- entity entity:<5> [USER]: User; The user interacting with this agent.

            Retrieved episodes:
            none

Decide whether the retrieved memory context is sufficient to answer the question in the same core direction as the noisy expected-answer label.
Retrieved memory context is the only source of truth. The expected-answer label is a comparison hint, not evidence.
Mark supported=true when the context contains equivalent source-grounded facts, preferences, events, or rationale, even if wording differs.
For imported chat transcripts, source-grounded support may come from adjacent turns and the active topic, not only from one sentence that literally repeats the answer.
Accept a strong conversational entailment when the expected answer follows from the surrounding user/assistant turns and no other retrieved source points to a competing answer.
Details that appear only in the expected-answer label but not in retrieved memory are non-binding and must not be called central unless the retrieved context itself makes them necessary for answering the question.
The official expected answer can contain noisy illustrative examples or broad inferences not literally present in the source. Do not require every illustrative example when the memory supports the central answer.
For preference or personalization questions, supported=true when the retrieved memory contains the concrete user-specific consideration needed to personalize the answer in the same core direction, even if the expected answer lists additional absent examples.
Do not mark unsupported solely because the official answer names an extra participant, pet name, or causal detail that is absent from the retrieved source while the central remembered event, preference, count, or rationale is still supported.
Mark supported=false when the context is missing the central answer, contradicts it, leaves several plausible competing answers, or only supports a weaker unrelated subset.
